expectations.text: Brief includes the full idempotent .sql DDL (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS + indexes + RLS + migration tracker INSERT)
expectations.evidence: §4 of brief.md contains full DDL: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS abc_os.build_resources` (lines 80-88), `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_build_resources_project_id` + `_project_sort` (lines 91-94), `ALTER TABLE ... ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY` in DO block with EXCEPTION handler (lines 97-103), `DROP POLICY IF EXISTS` + `CREATE POLICY` (lines 106-111), and migration tracker `INSERT INTO abc_os._aspace_migrations ... ON CONFLICT (filename) DO NOTHING` (lines 114-116). Wrapped in BEGIN/COMMIT for atomicity. Idempotent: every DDL uses IF NOT EXISTS or drop-and-recreate.

expectations.text: Brief includes the apply-abc-os-migrations.sh update (pre-flight list + apply_sql block for both dev and prod branches)
expectations.evidence: §5 of brief.md covers all 3 edit points: §5a pre-flight file list (add `"0013_build_resources.sql"` to the `for f in ...` array around line 53), §5b apply_sql dev branch (line 127), §5c apply_sql prod branch (line 141). Both branches get the same `apply_sql "0013_build_resources.sql"` line, with rationale: 'Shared-catalog DDL goes to both branches (schema + RLS, no seed)'.

expectations.text: Brief includes the 5 standard D1 verify queries + at least 2 build_resources-specific spot-checks
expectations.evidence: §7a lists all 5 standard D1 verify queries: schema_exists, table_count, rls_enabled_count, policy_count, migrations_logged. §7b lists 3 build_resources-specific spot-checks (exceeds the 2-minimum threshold): (1) new table exists in abc_os schema, (2) RLS enabled + exactly 1 policy named build_resources_*, (3) FK constraint to build_projects exists. §7c has Expected vs Actual template for sub-agent to fill.

expectations.text: Brief cites doctrine anchors (D1-D8, D10 mixed-tenancy) and uses FORCE_TARGET=1 with ON CONFLICT safety net
expectations.evidence: §1 cites D1-D8 + D10 explicitly with parenthetical anchors ('ADR-META-001', 'ADR-ABCOS-001', 'ADR-SUPABASE-001'). §6 mandates `FORCE_TARGET=1 ./scripts/apply-abc-os-migrations.sh dev`. ON CONFLICT safety net in §4 line 116: `ON CONFLICT (filename) DO NOTHING`. §2 tenancy table explicitly classifies build_resources as 'shared catalog' with RLS shape 'select-authenticated only' per D10.

expectations.text: Brief specifies the 3-file write-back contract (wiki/log.md, MEMORY_CORE/README.md, handoff)
expectations.evidence: §8 specifies all 3 files in order: §8a `00_Amadeus/30_MEMORY_CORE/LLM_Wiki/wiki/log.md` (prepend `## 2026-06-12` block with D1 verify receipts), §8b `00_Amadeus/30_MEMORY_CORE/README.md` (one-line bullet at top), §8c `00_Amadeus/30_MEMORY_CORE/LLM_Wiki/wiki/hand_offs/handoff_abc_os_community_dev_2026-06-10.md` (append P_N Backend Status section). Each includes the exact markdown block template the sub-agent should use.

expectations.text: Brief includes expected post-apply counts (tables=18, RLS=18, policies=43, migrations_logged=13) so sub-agent can compare to actual
expectations.evidence: §7a comments show expected values per query (tables=18, RLS=18, policies=43, migrations_logged=13). §7c has a 8-row 'Expected vs Actual' table the sub-agent fills. §12 has a full 'Expected post-apply counts' reference card: tables=18, RLS=18, policies=43, migrations=13, build_resources table=1, policies on build_resources=1, FKs to build_projects=1. All match A0's intent.

expectations.text: [NEW-1] Brief uses the canonical migration tracker table `abc_os._aspace_migrations` (not fictional `abc_os.migrations` or `abc_os.schema_migrations` or similar) with `ON CONFLICT (filename) DO NOTHING`
expectations.evidence: §4 line 114-116: `INSERT INTO abc_os._aspace_migrations (filename) VALUES ('0013_build_resources.sql') ON CONFLICT (filename) DO NOTHING;` — canonical shape from 0002_rls_policies.sql precedent. §3 baseline (line 60): `SELECT count(*) FROM abc_os._aspace_migrations;`. §7a Q5 (line 202): same canonical query. No fictional `abc_os.migrations` or `abc_os.schema_migrations` anywhere in the brief.

expectations.text: [NEW-2] Brief does NOT hard-fail on FORCE_TARGET=1 in the prod branch of the apply script (D10 shared-catalog applies to both envs; the apply script's prod gate is a separate `GO-PROD` HITL prompt, not a FORCE_TARGET ban)
expectations.evidence: §5c line 145-151: prod branch gets the same `apply_sql "0013_build_resources.sql"` line as dev. Rationale on line 153: 'Shared-catalog DDL goes to both branches (schema + RLS, no seed)'. The brief correctly distinguishes FORCE_TARGET=1 (env-var override) from the prod HITL gate (`GO-PROD` prompt in apply-abc-os-migrations.sh line 90-95). No 'exit 3' / 'FORCE_TARGET forbidden' / 'hard-fail' pattern in the brief.

claims.claim: build_resources is shared catalog (D10), RLS = select-authenticated only
claims.evidence: §2 tenancy classification table + §4 RLS policy `FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING (true)`. D10 mixed-tenancy precedent from ADR-ABCOS-001.

claims.claim: FORCE_TARGET=1 bypasses prod app.env mismatch per 2026-06-12 precedent
claims.evidence: §6 cites 'precedent 2026-06-12' for FORCE_TARGET=1 + ON CONFLICT safety net. The brief correctly adds the new migration to BOTH dev and prod branches (§5b/§5c) — the apply script's prod HITL gate is the `GO-PROD` prompt, not a FORCE_TARGET ban.
§12 reference card matches the deltas implied by adding 1 new shared-catalog table with 1 RLS policy + 1 migration tracker INSERT.

suggestions.reason: 8 assertions now catch the canonical migration tracker table name (NEW-1) and the FORCE_TARGET=1 prod-branch anti-pattern (NEW-2). Brief passes all 8 cleanly.

eval_feedback.overall: Brief is excellent — comprehensive, doctrine-aware, all expected counts accurate. The 2 new anti-doctrinal-error guards pass on this brief, validating that the skill's guidance is sufficient for sub-agents to produce canonically-correct SQL + apply script edits.
